blocks.text: Wisdom prospered their works by the hand of a holy prophet.
They journeyed through an uninhabited wilderness
They withstood their enemies and fought off their foes.
When they were thirsty, they called upon you,
For through the very things by which their enemies were punished,
Instead of the fountain of an ever-flowing river,
in rebuke for the decree to kill the infants,
showing by their thirst at that time
For when they were tried, though they were being disciplined in mercy,
For you tested them as a parent does in warning,
Whether absent or present, they were equally distressed,
for a twofold grief possessed them
For when they heard that through their own punishments
For though they had mockingly rejected him who long before had been cast out and exposed,
In return for their foolish and wicked thoughts,
so that they might learn that one is punished by the very things by which one sins.
For your all-powerful hand,
or newly created unknown beasts full of rage
not only could the harm they did destroy people,
Even apart from these, people could fall at a single breath
For it is always in your power to show great strength,
Because the whole world before you is like a speck that tips the scales
But you are merciful to all, for you can do all things,
For you love all things that exist
How would anything have endured if you had not willed it?
You spare all things, for they are yours, O Lord, you who love the living.
